compiled_state.author: Iso Tester
compiled_state.description:
## Why

Epic 3156 required a **visual-editor UX** for authoring project criteria (story 6e31). That UX is
BROKEN for the primary case — authoring a NET-NEW criterion — so the deliverable's user experience
fails a round trip:

- A project criterion MUST be `project.<name>`-prefixed (the overlay namespace that guarantees a
  project criterion can never rebind a built-in — ef7e/ADR 0015).
- Its rubric is a prompt stored at `.rebar/prompts/plan-review-<id>.md`, and `get_prompt` /
  `create_prompt` derive that filename from the criterion id as `plan-review-<id>`.
- But `prompt_library._validate_id` → `prompt_authoring._valid_id` enforces `[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9-]*`
  — **no `.`** (a filename-safety rule: a `.` would let an id masquerade as a `<id>.<variant>.md`
  overlay and enables traversal). So `plan-review-project.foo` is rejected at author time, AND
  `.rebar/prompts/` would mis-read a dotted stem as a variant overlay.

Net effect: the editor can RE-TUNE a built-in (id `F1`) but CANNOT author a net-new `project.<name>`
criterion end-to-end — the exact thing project-supplied criteria exist for. 6e31 currently
validate-then-rollback-rejects the attempt with a clean 400, so it never persists a broken overlay,
but the UX still dead-ends. This must be remediated for the editor UX to actually work.

## What — decouple the logical id from the physical filename (the Semgrep/npm/Python pattern)

Popular, actively-maintained tools NEVER force a namespaced logical id to also be a raw filename;
they decouple via ONE deterministic mapping:
- **Semgrep**: the dotted rule `id` is a metadata field, decoupled from the file name.
- **npm**: `@scope/name` → a deterministic path `node_modules/@scope/name/`.
- **Python**: `a.b.c` → `a/b/c.py` (dots → path separators).

Apply the same here. The criterion id stays `project.<name>` (dotted) EVERYWHERE it matters
(overlay key, `activate`, routing, no-rebind collision check, the `.`→`_` workflow-output-key
sanitization). Only the RUBRIC's physical storage is decoupled, via a SINGLE canonical function:

- Add `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id) -> str` (one source of truth): a built-in `<id>` →
  `plan-review-<id>` (unchanged); a `project.<name>` → `plan-review-project-<name>` (`.`→`-`), a
  filesystem-safe, `_valid_id`-valid prompt id. Injective because a project name is constrained to
  a DOT-FREE charset (exactly one namespace dot) — validate this in `_validate_routing_entry` /
  the namespace check so `project.a-b` and any dotted variant can never collide.
- Route BOTH sides through it so they can never diverge:
  - descriptor resolution: `criteria.model.build_descriptor` / `plan_review.registry` resolve a
    project criterion's rubric via `get_prompt(criterion_prompt_id(cid), repo_root=...)` (not the
    raw `plan-review-<cid>`);
  - editor authoring: `criterion_preview.author_criterion_overlay` writes the rubric via
    `create_prompt(criterion_prompt_id(cid), ...)` (now a valid id → passes `_valid_id`), then the
    atomic overlay routing+activate write keyed by the dotted `project.<name>` id.
- `enumerate_library` / `_user_prompt_ids`: a `plan-review-project-<name>.md` stem has no `.`, so it
  is enumerated normally (no variant-overlay mis-read). No change needed beyond using the sanitized
  filename.

## Scope

- Files: `src/rebar/llm/criteria/model.py` (or a small shared `ids.py`) — `criterion_prompt_id`;
  `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py` + `src/rebar/llm/criteria/model.py` (descriptor rubric
  resolution); `src/rebar/llm/workflow/criterion_preview.py` (authoring writes via the mapping);
  `src/rebar/llm/criteria/overlay.py` or `_validate_routing_entry` (constrain project name to
  dot-free); the editor UI (`editor_assets/src/rebarProvider.jsx`) so the authoring form derives +
  shows the resulting criterion id.
- **No back-compat burden (just-shipped feature):** project criteria shipped in ef7e days ago with
  no external data/users, so backward compatibility is NOT a requirement — the sanitized form is
  simply THE form. One clean code path (no dotted-`get_prompt` fallback). The ef7e/overlay TEST
  FIXTURES that hand-place a dotted `plan-review-project.no_print.md` are just rewritten to the
  sanitized `project.no-print` → `plan-review-project-no-print.md` — a free edit, not a migration.
  Built-in prompt-id resolution is unchanged only because there is no reason to touch it, not for
  compat.
- **Project-name charset (RESOLVED):** a project criterion name follows the SAME rule as any prompt
  id — `[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9-]*` (alnum + dash; NO dots, NO underscores) — with exactly ONE
  namespace dot in the full id (`project.<name>`). This makes `criterion_prompt_id` a total,
  injective, filesystem-safe map (`.`→`-` on the single namespace dot). A name containing `.` or
  `_` (or otherwise not matching the charset) is a LOCATED error at overlay load.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A single canonical `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id)` maps `project.<name>` →
      `plan-review-project-<name>` (filesystem-safe, passes `_valid_id`) and a built-in `<id>` →
      `plan-review-<id>` (unchanged); it is the ONLY id→prompt-filename derivation, used by BOTH
      descriptor resolution and editor authoring.
- [ ] A net-new `project.<name>` criterion can be authored end-to-end via the editor
      (`create_prompt` no longer rejects it) AND then loaded/run by the gate (`load_criteria`
      resolves its rubric) — a test proves the full author→activate→load→run round trip.
- [ ] Project names are constrained to a dot-free charset (one namespace dot) so the id→prompt-id
      map is injective; a name with a `.` is a located error.
- [ ] `criterion_prompt_id` is total + injective: a built-in `<id>` → `plan-review-<id>` and a
      `project.<name>` → ONLY `plan-review-project-<name>` (no dotted fallback); a dotted/underscored
      project name is a located error. (Back-compat is not a requirement — one clean path.)
- [ ] The editor authoring form surfaces the resulting criterion id (`project.<name>`) so the user
      sees what will be created; a net-new author no longer dead-ends at a 400.


## The dotted criterion id flows through explicitly (remediation, run 2)

The mapping `criterion_prompt_id` is ONE-WAY (`project.foo` → `plan-review-project-foo`); it is NOT
reversible (a name may contain dashes, so `plan-review-project-foo-bar` can't be un-split to a unique
dotted id). Therefore the DOTTED criterion id (`project.foo`) is the primary key and must be carried
explicitly — never reverse-derived from the sanitized prompt id:
- The editor authoring form has an explicit **criterion-id field** whose value IS the dotted id
  (`project.<name>`); the `/library/create` POST body carries BOTH `id` = the dotted criterion id AND
  the `routing` object. (Today `_library_create` strips `plan-review-` off the prompt id to recover
  `cid` — that path only works for built-ins; for a project criterion the dotted id comes from the
  form field, not from un-stripping.)
- `author_criterion_overlay(criterion_id, routing, *, repo_root)` takes the DOTTED `criterion_id` as
  its primary argument. It computes `prompt_id = criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id)` for the
  `create_prompt(prompt_id, rubric)` write, and keys the overlay `plan_review[criterion_id]` +
  `activate` entries by the DOTTED id. So the sanitized prompt filename is derived once, forward-only;
  the dotted id is authoritative for the overlay + activation.
- `write_criterion_overlay(repo_root, criterion_id, routing)` likewise keys by the dotted id.

## Acceptance Criteria (remediation addition, run 2)

- [ ] The dotted criterion id (`project.<name>`) is passed EXPLICITLY through authoring (the editor
      form's id field → `/library/create` body → `author_criterion_overlay(criterion_id=...)`), never
      reverse-derived from the sanitized prompt id; the overlay entry + `activate` are keyed by the
      dotted id while the rubric is written at `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id)`.


## Call-site responsibility (remediation, run 3 — the single wiring table)

Exactly one site applies `criterion_prompt_id`; every site's job is pinned:

1. **Editor form** (`rebarProvider.jsx`): the id field's value IS the dotted criterion id
   (`project.<name>`). POST `/library/create` body = `{id: "project.<name>", kind: "criterion",
   body: <rubric md>, routing: {...}}`.
2. **`editor._library_create`** (the orchestrator): for `kind == "criterion"` with a `routing`, it
   (a) computes `prompt_id = criteria.ids.criterion_prompt_id(id)`; (b) calls
   `create_prompt(prompt_id, body, repo_root=...)` — THIS is the ONE place `criterion_prompt_id` is
   applied before `create_prompt`, so the sanitized (valid) id reaches the write path; (c) calls
   `write_criterion_overlay(repo_root, criterion_id=id, routing)` keyed by the DOTTED id (routing +
   `activate`). Order is prompt-first (a failed overlay leaves an inactive, harmless prompt), and the
   overlay write is the existing atomic validate-then-rollback.
3. **`author_criterion_overlay`** is RENAMED/refocused to take the dotted `criterion_id` (not a
   prompt id) — it no longer strips `plan-review-` to recover the id (that path only worked for
   built-ins and can't recover a dotted id from the sanitized one). It delegates the routing+activate
   write to `write_criterion_overlay(repo_root, criterion_id, routing)`.
4. **Descriptor resolution** (`plan_review/registry._get_prompt` at registry.py:236 + the shared
   `criteria.model.build_descriptor` prompt path): resolve a criterion's rubric via
   `get_prompt(criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id), repo_root=...)` instead of the raw
   `f"plan-review-{cid}"` — so a `project.<name>` reads `plan-review-project-<name>.md`.
5. **`criterion_prompt_id`** lives in a new tiny `src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py` (the single source),
   imported by both the editor authoring path and the descriptor path so they can never diverge.

## Acceptance Criteria (remediation addition, run 3)

- [ ] `criterion_prompt_id` is applied at exactly ONE pre-`create_prompt` site
      (`editor._library_create` for authoring) and at the descriptor-resolution site
      (`registry._get_prompt` / `build_descriptor`); a test authors `project.<name>` via
      `/library/create` and asserts the rubric lands at `.rebar/prompts/plan-review-project-<name>.md`
      AND `load_criteria` resolves it.

file_impact.reason: author_criterion end-to-end + author_criterion_overlay takes dotted id
routing-form surfaces criterion id + derived rubric path

compiled_state.title: Editor round-trip for net-new project criteria: decouple criterion id from rubric filename (project.<name> can't be authored — create_prompt forbids '.')

verify_commands.dd_text: net-new project criterion round-trips end-to-end + criterion_prompt_id mapping

verify_commands.command: python -m pytest tests/unit/test_criteria_overlay.py tests/unit/test_criteria_eval.py tests/unit/test_det_invariants.py tests/unit/test_attestation_invalidation.py tests/unit/test_unified_criteria.py -q
